fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.fonds_id: Alpine Club of Canada fonds (3,084 records)
fonds.scope: Administrative records, executive minutes, camp records (1906-2017), 42 metres textual records, 12,500 photographs, 85 albums
fonds.findings: ACC founding records 1906; 1906 Yoho camp administrative documentation; Parker served as first Secretary; Photograph: Elizabeth Parker reading her secretary report at Lake O'Hara camp (1909, photographer Frank Freeborn, M200/V14/S6)
fonds.notes: Primary archival repository for ACC materials. Founded 1906 by Wheeler and Parker. Estimated 3,084 records total in the collection.

fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: 53 diaries (1885-1944), 66 photograph albums (~5,000 images), scrapbooks with correspondence, Alpine Club records
fonds.findings: Wheeler-Parker founding correspondence (late 1905–early 1906); 1906 camp records; diaries document first ACC camp and Parker's role as secretary; photographs from early camps including 1906 Yoho camp
fonds.notes: Co-founder correspondence with Elizabeth Parker (M.T. initials signature). Diaries span entire founding period and first camp.

fonds.institution: Winnipeg Free Press / Manitoba Free Press Archives
fonds.fonds_id: MFP 1874-1931 (digitized via Newspapers.com & Winnipeg Public Library)
fonds.fonds_title: Manitoba Free Press / Winnipeg Free Press Archive
fonds.scope: 2 million+ digitized pages (1874-1931); searchable by name, keyword, date; full run of Parker's column 'A Reader's Notes' (Jan 1904–July 1940)
fonds.findings: Editorial advocating Canadian alpine club (March 1906, date precise to verify); Parker column as 'The Bookman'; founding advocacy writings against American Alpine Club subsidiary; camp reports and coverage
fonds.notes: Searchable online through Newspapers.com and Winnipeg Public Library card. Parker began career Jan 13, 1904.

fonds.scope: Unknown — likely personal papers, family records, correspondence
fonds.findings: Archives exists; no online finding aid confirms Elizabeth Parker fonds; likely holdings in provincial records
fonds.notes: Requires direct contact with Archives staff. Keystone Archives Descriptive Database searchable online but no specific Elizabeth Parker fonds description located.

fonds.institution: University of Manitoba Archives & Special Collections
fonds.scope: Unknown — likely papers, correspondence, editorial materials
fonds.findings: No confirmed Elizabeth Parker fonds located in online catalog. Matthew A. Parker fonds exists but distinct individual.
fonds.notes: Contact: archives@umanitoba.ca or (204) 474-9986. Not all collections digitized.

digital_assets_downloaded.caption: Elizabeth Parker reading her secretary report at Alpine Club of Canada annual meeting, Lake O'Hara, Yoho Park, B.C., 1909. Photographer: Frank W. Freeborn. Whyte Museum M200/V14/S6.
digital_assets_downloaded.license: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies (access by request)
